title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model ranks fourth on LM Arena among open models while being 2-20x smaller than competitors at similar quality. Variants run on single GPUs, mobile phones (2B and 4B), and local machines via LM Studio, with an Apache 2.0 license enabling adoption by sovereign institutions without procurement delays.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma's open model lineup, framing it around ownership and deployment without procurement delays. The 31B model ranks fourth among open models on LM Arena while running 2-20x smaller than competitors at similar quality, with variants that fit single GPUs, mobile phones (2B and 4B), and local machines via LM Studio. The Apache 2.0 license is the key enabler, allowing sovereign institutions to adopt the models freely. The takeaway: competitive open weights at multiple sizes, plus permissive licensing, support self-hosted and on-device use.
